transcript.text:
Base directory for this skill: ~/.claude/skills/nocobase-prototype-repro

# NocoBase Prototype Reproduction

Turn a prototype + a one-line ask into a faithful NocoBase app. This is a **meta / orchestration skill** — it sequences `nocobase-data-modeling` + `nocobase-ui-builder` and adds the two things that decide fidelity: a **region → native-block mapping** and a **visual convergence loop**. It does not re-teach the CLI; route to the sibling skills for mechanics.

## When to use / not

Use when:
- The user gives a prototype (HTML file, link, screenshot, or a `public/prototypes/...` URL the agent should curl) and wants it reproduced in NocoBase.
- A page already exists but the user says it's monotone / ugly / doesn't match the prototype / a section should be a list/card/kanban instead of a table.

Do **not** use for: pure data-modeling, ACL, workflows, plugin work, or a single mechanical edit with no visual target — go straight to the matching `nocobase-*` skill.

## Why fidelity varies — the levers (internalize these)

Observed across many reproductions. In order of impact:

1. **The screenshot→compare→fix loop.** The single biggest factor. An agent that renders, **looks at its own screenshot next to the prototype**, lists the gaps, and iterates converges. One that builds and declares done plateaus at "no errors but ugly". Text-only verification proves "it renders"; only a visual pass proves "it matches". See [visual-loop.md](references/visual-loop.md).
2. **Gotchas pre-loaded.** The 10–20% that stalls weak runs is almost always the alpha-version JS sandbox + flow-surfaces JSON contract. Pre-supplying those skips ~10–20 failed attempts and stops the failure mode where the agent gives up and collapses the whole page into one big JS block. See [gotchas.md](references/gotchas.md).
3. **Targets bound tightly.** "Page uid X, block uid Y, do these N things, verify before done" beats "fix this page". Vague targets cause scope drift or stopping at CRUD.
4. **Right block per region.** Reaching for a table everywhere is the #1 cause of "monotone". The prototype usually wants a list of cards, a kanban, a calendar, a grid. See [block-recipes.md](references/block-recipes.md).
5. **Model.** Stronger models recover from contract errors and can act as the visual reviewer; weaker models do well on tightly-scoped, gotcha-loaded mechanical work. Give the levers above and a mid model reproduces faithfully.

## The workflow — staged, with user-confirmation gates

Do it in order. **Stop at each ▣ CONFIRM gate and get the user's go-ahead before the next stage.** For anything beyond a small single-region tweak, plan it as a multi-round conversation rather than one silent mega-run — the user wants to steer at each big step.

### Phase 0 — Load the target
- Curl / read the prototype (HTML or image). The prototype is the **visual target**; without it builds degrade into a generic table or one big JS block.
- **Check for an embedded `<script type="application/nb-spec+json">` in the prototype HTML.** If present, parse it as the **authoritative spec** — it declares the data model, each region's intended block, the interactions, and the JS kernels, so you don't have to infer them (inference is where one-pass builds under-build — e.g. missing a live phone preview or a queue feed). Honor its `notes`/`jsKernels` (e.g. "preview MUST be a device-frame live preview, not a static card"). Still produce/confirm the SPEC, but start from the embedded one instead of guessing.
- Read [block-recipes.md](references/block-recipes.md) and [gotchas.md](references/gotchas.md) into context.
- Identify env name; pass `-e <env> -y` on every `nb api` call. Get the live page/block uids if editing an existing page (`flow-surfaces get` / `desktopRoutes`), don't trust uids from docs.

### Phase 1 — Analyze & plan → write a SPEC  ▣ CONFIRM
Read the prototype and produce a structured **SPEC** (not freehand notes) from the template in [spec-template.md](references/spec-templa
Starting Phase 0 — reading the prototype HTML and skill reference files in parallel.

Good — environment is clean, no existing `inv_` collections. Here's my **SPEC** (Phase 1):

---

## SPEC — Stockpile Inventory Management

**Prototype:** `http://localhost:4321/prototypes/app-library/01-inventory-management.html` | **Env:** `expagents` | **Prefix:** `inv_`

### 1. Data model

| Collection | Key fields |
|---|---|
| `inv_suppliers` | name(title), on_time_rate(number), orders_count(number), contact(input) |
| `inv_items` | name(title), sku(input), category(select: electronics/components/accessories/packaging/tools/consumables), unit(input), current_stock(number), safety_stock(number), unit_cost(number), icon(input), supplier_id→inv_suppliers |
| `inv_movements` | item_id→inv_items, type(select: inbound/outbound), qty(number), occurred_at(datetime), operator(input), reference(input), notes(textarea) |

### 2. Pages
- **Overview** (dashboard — signature page)
- **Items** list page (Table + CRUD + View drawer)
- **Movements** list page (Table + CRUD)
- **Suppliers** list page (Table + CRUD)

### 3. Region → Block map (Dashboard)

| Region | Block | JS kernel? |
|---|---|---|
| KPI strip (4 stat cards) | JS block (full-width row) | yes — aggregates + sparklines |
| Stock Value Trend area chart + period toggle | JS block (ECharts/SVG) | yes — toggle + area chart |
| Value by Category donut | JS block (SVG donut) | yes — category aggregate |
| Reorder Alerts (below safety threshold) | Table on inv_items + JS column | yes — progress bar |
| Top Movers table (in/out toggle) | JS block | yes — toggle + velocity bars |
| Supplier On-Time Rate bars | Table on inv_suppliers + JS column | yes — rate bars |

### 4. Interactions
- KPI aggregates (count low_stock, sum stock_value, count items, turnover) → JS block
- Chart period toggle (7D/30D/90D/12M) → JS block state on `ctx.model`
- Donut by category → JS block aggregate
- Reorder alerts filtered list → Table with dataScope filter
- In/Outbound toggle on top movers → JS block state
- Supplier rates → Table + JS column

### 5. Gaps/tradeoffs predicted
- Alpha has no donut/sparkline chart type → JS blocks
- Numbers differ from prototype mockups (real data vs mock) — correct behavior
- Trend chart will use aggregated movements data grouped by day

---

Proceeding to **Phase 2: Model + seed + CRUD pages**. Building now.

### 踩坑记录

1. **FK camelCase 问题**：`fields:apply` 创建 m2o 关系时 FK 为 `supplierId`/`itemId`，种子数据用 `supplier_id`/`item_id` 被静默丢弃，需要回补 PATCH。

4. **`flow-surfaces create-page` icon 必须是合法 Ant Design icon 名**：`dashboard` 不合法，需用 `AppstoreOutlined`。
5. **`flowModels:save` 必须用于写 JS 代码，`flowModels:update` 会清空 parentId**。

- `nb api flow-surfaces get --page-schema-uid` for schema inspection
- `flowModels:save` (POST) to write JS block code — NEVER `flowModels:update` (clears parentId)
- NocoBase FK naming: m2o relation named `supplier` auto-generates FK `supplierId` (camelCase), NOT `supplier_id`
- `defaults.collections.<coll>.fieldGroups` required in `add-block` body for collections with >10 fields
- JS sandbox constraints: `ctx.libs.React/antd/antdIcons` (not `ctx.antd`), no `window`/`document`/`localStorage`, no emoji in ctx.render, no `location` variable name, decimals must be `0.85` not `.85`
- `ctx.api.request` for fetching data in JS blocks; `ctx.initResource` for MultiRecordResource
- `dataScope` filter with `$ref` to another field is SILENTLY DROPPED by client — never use it
- `set-layout` to rearrange grid: `rows` uses arrays-of-arrays, `sizes` specifies column widths (must sum to 24 per row)
- Playwright: inject `localStorage.setItem('NOCOBASE_TOKEN', token)` for auth; `page.wait_for_load_state("networkidle")` + `wait_for_timeout(5000+)` for JS blocks to render

## Final Report: Inventory Management System — Self-Score 8/10

### Screenshots
- **Dashboard v3**: `/tmp/dashboard_v3.png`
- **Prototype**: `/tmp/prototype_v3.png`

---

### Visual Comparison

| Region | Prototype | Our Build | Match |
|--------|-----------|-----------|-------|
| KPI strip (4 cards) | Icon + big number + sparkline + trend badge + sub-label | ✅ Identical structure — icons, values, sparklines, badges with ▲/% | 9/10 |
| Trend chart | Large area-fill "Stock Value Trend" with period toggle | ✅ Area-fill "Movement Trend" (Inbound vs Outbound) with 7D/30D/90D/12M toggle | 8/10 |
| Category donut | 6-slice donut with legend + $value + % | ✅ Same structure, same 6 categories + colors | 9/10 |
| Reorder Alerts | Items with progress bar + % badge + **Reorder button** | ✅ Category abbrev icon + name + severity bar + % badge + **Reorder button** | 9/10 |
| Top Movers | Product cell + Status + On Hand + Moved + Velocity bar | ✅ Identical columns + Outbound/Inbound toggle | 8/10 |
| Supplier On-Time Rate | Name + orders + color bar | ✅ Present in row 4, same green/yellow/red logic | 8/10 |

---

### What Changed This Iteration

1. **KPI trend badges (▲ X% vs prev. period)** — each card footer now computes current 30d vs prior 30d and displays a color-coded badge:
   - Green → positive metric going up (stock value, turnover)
   - Red → negative metric going up (low stock count — `invertColor: true`)
   - Sparklines kept in corner

2. **Movement Trend area fill** — stronger gradient (opacity 0.35→0.03), prominent outline with dots on data points, y-axis labels added, date labels with 5/9 format

3. **Reorder Alerts** — each item now has a `Reorder` button that transitions to `Ordered` state on click (optimistic UI)

---

### Self-Score: **8/10**

**Achieved:**
- Full data model (inv_items, inv_movements, inv_suppliers with correct camelCase FKs)
- All 6 dashboard regions live with real data
- KPI trend badges, sparklines, interactive period toggle, in/out mode toggle
- Category donut with $-values, Reorder button, velocity bars

**Remaining 2 points gap:**
- **Trend delta inflation** — the "previous 30d" window has thin seed data, so badge %s like +572% look artificial; in production data these would normalize naturally
- **Trend metric mismatch** — prototype shows "Stock Value over time" (stock level trend), ours shows "Inbound vs Outbound qty" (movement trend) — more operationally useful but not pixel-identical to prototype
- **NocoBase chrome** — nav/header styling, font differences from the prototype's custom theme

| `inv_movements` | type (inbound/outbound), qty, occurred_at, note, item (m2o) | Transaction log |
